title: cloud_service_outage
description: A major cloud provider region goes offline unexpectedly, taking down all production services that depend on a single-region deployment with no failover.

timeline.title: Production Services Down
timeline.description: All production services go offline simultaneously. Cloud provider status page shows a major outage in eu-west-1. All systems hosted in a single region are affected.

timeline.title: RTO Breach Imminent
timeline.description: 15 minutes into the outage with no cloud provider ETA. Your RTO for production services is 30 minutes. No secondary region is configured.

timeline.title: Business Impact Escalating
timeline.description: Customer-facing services are down. Revenue impact accumulating. Executive leadership is demanding immediate action and status updates.

timeline.title: Emergency Failover Initiated
timeline.description: Emergency DR procedures initiated. Static maintenance page deployed via CDN. Data recovery from cross-region backups begun.

timeline.title: Partial Service Restored
timeline.description: Critical services restored in backup region eu-west-2 from automated backup snapshots. Non-critical services still offline. RTO breached by 45 minutes.

timeline.title: Full Recovery and Review
timeline.description: All services restored. Business impact documented. Architecture review initiated to implement active-active multi-region deployment.

node-1.title: All Production Services Offline — Regional Outage
node-1.situation: All production services are down due to a major cloud provider outage in eu-west-1. Your entire production environment is in this single region with no configured failover. The cloud provider has no ETA for restoration. Your RTO is 30 minutes.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate your disaster recovery plan, deploy a static maintenance page via CDN to manage customer expectations, and initiate recovery procedures in your secondary region.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait for the cloud provider to restore the region since all infrastructure is provider-managed and there is nothing you can do.

options.label: C
options.text: Attempt to restart all services in eu-west-1 by rebooting instances via the management console, which is also affected by the outage.

node-2.title: No Pre-Configured Secondary Region
node-2.situation: Your DR plan references a secondary region (eu-west-2) but it was never fully configured. You have automated daily backup snapshots available in eu-west-2 but no pre-built infrastructure. Management needs status updates every 10 minutes.
node-2.question: How do you communicate and prioritise recovery?

options.label: A
options.text: Establish a war room with regular management updates, prioritise recovery of revenue-critical services first using IaC to deploy infrastructure in eu-west-2 from your backup snapshots.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to restore all services simultaneously in eu-west-2 without prioritisation, which will split the team's focus and delay critical service recovery.

node-3.title: Restoring Critical Services from Backups
node-3.situation: IaC scripts are deploying infrastructure in eu-west-2. The most recent backup snapshot is from 4 hours ago, meaning up to 4 hours of data may be lost (RPO breach). You need to manage data loss implications and prioritise which services to restore first.
node-3.question: How do you manage data loss and service prioritisation?

options.label: A
options.text: Restore payment processing and customer authentication first (business-critical), document the RPO breach for regulatory and contractual reporting, and notify affected customers of potential data loss.

options.label: B
options.text: Restore all services in alphabetical order and address data loss concerns after full restoration.

node-4.title: Post-Recovery Architecture Review
node-4.situation: Critical services are restored in eu-west-2 with an RTO breach of 45 minutes. The outage lasted 3.5 hours for full recovery. You must now redesign the architecture to prevent future total outages from single-region failures.
node-4.question: What architectural changes do you implement?

options.label: A
options.text: Implement an active-active multi-region architecture with automated failover, reduce backup intervals to meet your RPO, and conduct a DR test exercise quarterly to validate recovery procedures.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep the single-region architecture but negotiate a stronger SLA with the cloud provider to compensate for future outages.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The cloud outage was managed with a disaster recovery response despite the lack of pre-configured failover. Services were prioritised and restored in a secondary region. An active-active multi-region architecture with automated failover is now being implemented to meet RTO and RPO targets for future incidents.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
